mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates on future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment drops below 5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings up and pushing S&P 500 higher over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration, which expands profit margins and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative IC when INDPRO and HOUST diverge, or forward returns fail to outperform when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than priced in, compressing equity multiples and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation months, or inflation surprises consistently fail to predict rate-hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows zero correlation with subsequent equity returns, or negative sales months consistently precede positive SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job creation exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic momentum, boosting investor risk appetite and driving S&P 500 higher over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where job growth exceeds 200k, indicating the market prices the data as inflationary rather than growth-positive.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands alongside real GDP growth, supply constraints ease while aggregate demand remains strong, creating an optimal environment for corporate earnings expansion and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns when supply growth outpaces demand growth, or when the interaction term shows negative predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1.5 million units signal a sharp deceleration in construction activity and consumer confidence, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive despite sustained housing starts below 1.5 million, or housing data consistently leads equity declines by less than 30 days.
